dubois-souls-wings-of-atalanta.alt: Four African American women seated on the steps of a building at Atlanta University, Georgia, 1899 or 1900
dubois-souls-wings-of-atalanta.caption: Four young women on the steps at Atlanta University around 1900, from the photographs Du Bois assembled for the Paris Exposition, the institution whose students he casts as the wings bearing Black youth toward knowledge.
dubois-souls-wings-of-atalanta.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division (African American Photographs Assembled for the 1900 Paris Exposition)
dubois-souls-wings-of-atalanta.license: Public domain (no known restrictions on publication)

negro-problem-representative-negroes-dun.alt: Portrait photograph of Frederick Douglass, circa 1879
negro-problem-representative-negroes-dun.caption: Frederick Douglass around 1879, one of the representative Black American leaders Paul Laurence Dunbar profiles in his 1903 essay.
negro-problem-representative-negroes-dun.repository: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration (via Wikimedia Commons)

negro-problem-representative-negroes-dun.alt: Portrait photograph of the poet Paul Laurence Dunbar
negro-problem-representative-negroes-dun.caption: Paul Laurence Dunbar, author of the 1903 survey of representative Black Americans, photographed near the end of his life.
negro-problem-representative-negroes-dun.photographer: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division
negro-problem-representative-negroes-dun.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division

negro-problem-characteristics-kealing.alt: Reverend Henry Hugh Proctor, pastor of the First Congregational Church, Atlanta, Georgia, 1900
negro-problem-characteristics-kealing.caption: The Reverend Henry Hugh Proctor of Atlanta, photographed in 1900, reflecting the church leadership and religious life central to H. T. Kealing's account of the inner character of Black Americans.
negro-problem-characteristics-kealing.photographer: African American Photographs Assembled for the 1900 Paris Exposition (Daniel Murray Collection)
negro-problem-characteristics-kealing.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division

negro-problem-disfranchisement-chesnutt.alt: Wood engraving The First Vote, showing African American men waiting in line to cast ballots, Harper's Weekly, 1867
negro-problem-disfranchisement-chesnutt.caption: The First Vote (Harper's Weekly, 1867) depicts Black men exercising the franchise during Reconstruction, the very right whose legal stripping Charles W. Chesnutt documents in his 1903 essay on disfranchisement.
negro-problem-disfranchisement-chesnutt.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division

dubois-souls-coming-of-john.alt: Bazoline Estelle Usher, an Atlanta University student, half-length portrait, 1900
dubois-souls-coming-of-john.caption: An Atlanta University student photographed in 1900, evoking the Northern schooling that transforms and estranges Du Bois's young protagonist in Of the Coming of John.
dubois-souls-coming-of-john.photographer: African American Photographs Assembled for the 1900 Paris Exposition (Daniel Murray Collection)
dubois-souls-coming-of-john.repository: Library of Congress, Prints and Photographs Division
